mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase discount rates for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in 65% of observations despite rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real spending aggregates signals weakening consumer demand that forces corporate earnings revisions downward, driving negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during recession onset.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling nonfarm payrolls indicates structural labor market deterioration that reduces earnings growth expectations and suppresses equity prices.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or signal fails to predict direction in 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp rebounds in industrial production following supply chain disruptions restore capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% of recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent headline inflation combined with aggressive rate hikes forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, extending valuation compression and depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal predicts positive returns in 65% of triggered months, or inflation cools rapidly without triggering earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts reflect tightening mortgage credit and reduced household leverage capacity, transmitting financial stress to broader equity markets over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in 70% of declining housing start periods, or the correlation with SPX breaks down during rate pause cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate stabilization below 4.5% while payrolls remain positive confirms a soft landing scenario that removes recession risk premiums and supports equity valuations.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in 60% of soft landing instances, or unemployment spikes above 5% within the prediction window.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth in late-cycle expansions signals diminishing marginal returns to capital investment, triggering sector rotation away from cyclicals and negative index returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 65% of deceleration quarters, or GDP revisions reverse the initial signal within two weeks.
